text.title: function-calling-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for function-calling-patterns. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Execute independent tool calls from the same LLM response in parallel using ThreadPoolExecutor.
rule.rationale: When the LLM returns multiple tool calls in one response, they are often independent. Sequential execution wastes wall-clock time proportional to the number of tools called.

rule.statement: Execute tools with shared state or ordering constraints sequentially, not in parallel.
rule.rationale: Parallel execution of tools that read and write shared state produces race conditions. The LLM cannot express ordering constraints within a single tool_use block.

rule.statement: Validate tool arguments against the declared schema before calling the implementation. Reject invalid calls with a structured error in the tool_result.
rule.rationale: LLMs hallucinate argument values, especially enum values, IDs, and dates. Passing hallucinated args to a real implementation can cause data corruption or external API errors.

rule.statement: Return structured errors in tool_result with an "error" key and a "code" key (e.g., {"error": "not found", "code": "NOT_FOUND"}).
rule.rationale: A structured error format lets the model reason about what went wrong and decide the next action (retry, ask the user, use a different tool) rather than just seeing an opaque string.

rule.statement: Use additionalProperties: false in tool schemas to prevent the model from inventing extra fields.
rule.rationale: Without this constraint, models may add undeclared fields that your schema validator rejects or your implementation ignores silently. Strict schemas catch hallucinated fields early.

rule.statement: Truncate tool results to at most 2000 tokens before returning them to context. Summarize if needed.
rule.rationale: Large tool results (full DB rows, API responses) inflate context rapidly. After 5+ tool turns, un-truncated results can push the conversation past the context limit.

rule.statement: After 5+ tool turns in a multi-turn agent, consider summarizing earlier results to avoid hitting context limits.
rule.rationale: Without summarization, full tool results accumulate and consume most of the context window, leaving little room for new interactions.

rule.statement: Always enforce a max_iterations limit (10 or below in production). Log and alert when it triggers.
rule.rationale: Without a limit, an agent that repeatedly fails a tool call or cannot find a satisfactory answer loops indefinitely, consuming tokens and potentially causing side effects.

rule.statement: Continue the loop until stop_reason == "end_turn" or "max_tokens". stop_reason == "tool_use" means the task is not complete.
rule.rationale: Each tool_use stop_reason means the model wants to call a tool and continue. Treating tool_use as task completion returns a partial result and skips the model's intended next step.

rule.statement: When stop_reason == "max_tokens", raise an exception rather than returning a partial response.
rule.rationale: max_tokens means the response was truncated and the model may have intended to add more tool calls or text. Returning the incomplete response misleads the user.

rule.statement: Require explicit human approval before dispatching any tool that sends messages, modifies data, or charges money.
rule.rationale: Agentic loops can trigger destructive actions without user awareness. A human-in-loop checkpoint before irreversible operations is the only reliable safety gate in automated pipelines.

rule.statement: Implement dry-run mode for all tools with side effects. Always test agentic loops in dry-run before enabling live execution.
rule.rationale: Without dry-run, verifying loop behavior requires making real external changes. Dry-run allows safe iteration on the loop logic without production risk.

rule.statement: When a tool fails, return actionable error text in the tool_result. Avoid terse error messages that the model cannot reason about.
rule.rationale: The model decides its next action based on the tool_result content. A vague error like "500 Internal Server Error" gives no guidance. "order_id 12345 not found; valid IDs are 6-digit numbers" enables self-correction.

rule.statement: When you have more than ~10-15 tools, implement a router that selects the relevant tool subset based on the query before passing to the LLM.
rule.rationale: Model tool selection accuracy degrades when the tool list is large; a router that pre-filters the tool set reduces the decision space and improves accuracy.
